tool_args.label: Bronx Bus Network Redesign Final Plan
tool_args.raw_text: Bronx Bus Network Redesign Final Plan published October 2019 by MTA New York City Transit

payload.document_title: Bronx Bus Network Redesign Final Plan

tool_args.label: Bronx Bus Network Redesign
tool_args.raw_text: The Bronx Bus Network Redesign is New York City Transit's first borough-wide bus network redesign conducted as part of the Fast Forward Plan initiative to Reimagine the Bus Network.

payload.description: First borough-wide bus network redesign conducted as part of the Fast Forward Plan initiative. Publish Existing Conditions Report February 2019, Draft Plan May 2019, Final Plan October 2019.

tool_args.label: MTA New York City Transit
tool_args.raw_text: The Bronx Bus Network Redesign is New York City Transit's first borough-wide bus network redesign conducted as part of the Fast Forward Plan

tool_args.label: NYC Department of Transportation (NYCDOT)
tool_args.raw_text: As part of the Bronx Bus Network Redesign planning process, MTA and NYCDOT conducted an analysis of major Bronx corridors to identify streets where bus lanes and other bus priority treatments would provide the biggest benefit to Bronx bus riders.

payload.name: New York City Department of Transportation

tool_args.label: Average Bus Stop Spacing - Existing Bronx
tool_args.raw_text: In the Bronx, that average is slightly higher, at 882 feet.

tool_args.label: Average Bus Stop Spacing - Proposed Bronx
tool_args.raw_text: The average distance between stops in the Bronx is 1,092 feet in our Final Plan, bringing it in line with international standards.

tool_args.label: Average Stop Spacing NYC Existing
tool_args.raw_text: New York City has the shortest distances between its bus stops of any major city, with an average of 805 feet between stops

tool_args.label: International Stop Spacing Range
tool_args.raw_text: international peer transit systems around the world, which typically ranges from 1,000 to 1,680 feet

tool_args.label: Re-entry Traffic Delay Range
tool_args.raw_text: The waiting time for a bus to re-enter traffic can range from 20 seconds during off-peak travels times to more than one minute during peak rush hour.

payload.raw_value_text: 20 seconds during off-peak travels times to more than one minute during peak rush hour

tool_args.label: Finalized route modifications summary
tool_args.raw_text: Of the 46 Local, Limited, and SBS routes that operate in the Bronx or out of a Bronx bus depot, we finalized modifications to 16 routes, with two new routes added, the Bx25 and the M125.

payload.claim_text: Of the 46 Local, Limited, and SBS routes that operate in the Bronx or out of a Bronx bus depot, we finalized modifications to 16 routes, with two new routes added (Bx25 and M125)

tool_args.label: Express bus modifications summary
tool_args.raw_text: Of the 11 express bus routes that operate in the Bronx, we finalized modifications to six routes and introduced one new route.

payload.claim_text: Of the 11 express bus routes that operate in the Bronx, we finalized modifications to six routes and introduced one new route

tool_args.label: Bus priority corridor analysis
tool_args.raw_text: A total of 46 corridors were scored and ranked accordingly. NYCDOT will begin the planning process for these bus priority corridors with the release of this Plan. Outreach and implementation on the first set of corridors will start in 2020.

payload.claim_text: MTA and NYCDOT scored and ranked 46 corridors for bus priority improvements. NYCDOT will begin planning with release of this Plan. Outreach and implementation on first set of corridors will start in 2020.

tool_args.label: New Bus Lanes Treatment
tool_args.raw_text: New Bus Lanes - Bus lanes separate buses from general traffic, improving speed and reliability. They are typically located along the curb or 'offset' from the curb. Automated Bus Lane Enforcement (ABLE) is now active on all M15 SBS buses, and will roll out on all M14 SBS and B44 SBS buses by November of 2019.

payload.description: Bus lanes separate buses from general traffic, improving speed and reliability. Typically curb or offset located. Automated Bus Lane Enforcement (ABLE) cameras mounted on bus fronts capture evidence of vehicles blocking bus lanes.

tool_args.label: Upgraded Bus Lane Treatment
tool_args.raw_text: Upgraded Bus Lane - NYCDOT monitors all bus lanes and will upgrade bus lanes when warranted. This could include extending hours, painting bus lanes red, changing curbside lanes to offset lanes.

payload.description: Extending hours of bus lane enforcement, painting bus lanes red, changing curbside lanes to offset lanes, and other measures.

tool_args.label: Protected Bus Lane Treatment
tool_args.raw_text: Protected Bus Lane - Using barriers to protect a bus lane makes it more difficult for vehicles other than buses to illegally use the lane.

payload.description: Using barriers to protect a bus lane makes it more difficult for vehicles other than buses to illegally use the lane.

tool_args.label: Transit and Freight Priority Street Treatment
tool_args.raw_text: A transit and freight priority street provides dedicated space for buses and trucks to traverse a corridor while limiting other through traffic.

payload.description: Provides dedicated space for buses and trucks to traverse a corridor while limiting other through traffic. May include curb regulation changes and turn restrictions.

tool_args.label: Bus Boarders Treatment
tool_args.raw_text: Bus bulbs are permanent sidewalk extensions at bus stops that provide more space for waiting passengers and allow buses to pull up to the curb without leaving the travel lane.

payload.description: Bus bulbs are permanent sidewalk extensions at bus stops. NYCDOT also uses durable recycled plastic 'bus boarders' that serve the same purpose without capital construction.

tool_args.label: Bus Queue Jump Lane and Transit Signal Priority Treatment
tool_args.raw_text: These bus queue jump signals allow a bus to enter an intersection before regular traffic and bypass waiting queues of traffic at a light, giving the bus a head start. Bus queue jump signals are often paired with bus lanes.

payload.description: Dedicated bus signal phases allow a bus to enter an intersection before regular traffic and bypass waiting queues. Often paired with bus lanes.

tool_args.label: New route Bx25
tool_args.raw_text: Bx25 - new route to operate between Bedford Park and north Co-op City via Allerton Avenue

tool_args.label: New route M125
tool_args.raw_text: M125 - 8-or-better new route with 8 min peak frequency, 60 min overnight, 24 hour service

payload.claim_text: M125 is a new route with 8-or-better frequency category, 24-hour service

tool_args.label: Grand Concourse express service maintained weekdays peak-only
tool_args.raw_text: Service on Grand Concourse will be maintained on weekdays during peak periods only. Also, we will no longer pursue express bus service between Co-op City and Lower Manhattan.

payload.claim_text: Service on Grand Concourse will be maintained on weekdays during peak periods only

tool_args.label: All-door boarding by 2021
tool_args.raw_text: All-door boarding will be launched on all bus routes by 2021.

payload.claim_text: All-door boarding will be launched on all bus routes by 2021

tool_args.label: OMNY payment system available on all buses and subways by end of 2020
tool_args.raw_text: The new OMNY payment system, available on all buses and subways by the end of 2020, will allow customers to make tap-and-go fare payments.

payload.claim_text: OMNY payment system available on all buses and subways by end of 2020

tool_args.label: Free transfer policy for riders impacted by redesign
tool_args.raw_text: Our policy: No rider should have to pay an additional transfer fare as a result of the Bronx Bus Network Redesign.

payload.claim_text: No rider should have to pay an additional transfer fare as a result of the Bronx Bus Network Redesign. The agency will seek to strategically add additional free transfers to the system.

tool_args.label: RTPI signs installed in Bronx since 2013
tool_args.raw_text: Since 2013, NYCDOT has installed 17 RTPI signs at non-SBS bus stops in the Bronx, all funded by the New York City Council.

tool_args.label: Bx6 SBS reroute via Story Avenue
tool_args.raw_text: As proposed in the Draft Plan, Bx6 SBS service will run along Bruckner Boulevard, Bronx River Avenue, and Story Avenue.

payload.claim_text: Bx6 SBS service will run along Bruckner Boulevard, Bronx River Avenue, and Story Avenue as proposed in Draft Plan

tool_args.label: Bx23 alignment unchanged
tool_args.raw_text: Bx23 - alignment will remain unchanged

payload.claim_text: Bx23 alignment will remain unchanged

tool_args.label: Bx28/38 maintain existing service in Co-op City
tool_args.raw_text: Bx28 - maintain existing service in Co-op City. Bx38 - maintain existing service in Co-op City.

payload.claim_text: Bx28 - maintain existing service in Co-op City. Bx38 - maintain existing service in Co-op City.

tool_args.label: ABLE rollout on M14 SBS and B44 SBS by Nov 2019
tool_args.raw_text: Automated Bus Lane Enforcement (ABLE) is now active on all M15 SBS buses, and will roll out on all M14 SBS and B44 SBS buses by November of 2019.

payload.raw_value_text: will roll out on all M14 SBS and B44 SBS buses by November of 2019

issues: metric_claim payload with raw_value_text should include normalized numeric value or value_min/value_max

tool_args.label: Bx40/42 maintain existing alignments in Throgs Neck
tool_args.raw_text: The southeastern route alignments of the Bx40 and Bx42 will remain as they exist today in Throgs Neck.

payload.claim_text: The southeastern route alignments of the Bx40 and Bx42 will remain as they exist today in Throgs Neck.

tool_args.label: Bx38 maintain existing alignment in Norwood
tool_args.raw_text: We will maintain the existing route alignment of the Bx38 to maintain service options for customers in Norwood traveling to/from Co-op City.

payload.claim_text: We will maintain the existing route alignment of the Bx38 to maintain service options for customers in Norwood traveling to/from Co-op City.

tool_args.label: Bx36, Bx40, Bx42 Tremont routing change
tool_args.raw_text: Bx36 will run on Tremont Avenue continuously between East 180 Street and Webster Avenue, and the Bx40 and Bx42 will run on East 180 Street between Tremont Avenue and Webster Avenue to allow for more direct routing.

payload.claim_text: Bx36 will run on Tremont Avenue continuously between East 180 Street and Webster Avenue, and Bx40 and Bx42 will run on East 180 Street between Tremont Avenue and Webster Avenue to allow for more direct routing.
payload.location: Tremont Avenue / East 180 Street / Webster Avenue

location_normalized.raw_text: Tremont Avenue / East 180 Street / Webster Avenue

tool_args.label: ABLE rollout on M14 SBS and B44 SBS by Nov 2019
tool_args.raw_text: Automated Bus Lane Enforcement (ABLE) is now active on all M15 SBS buses, and will roll out on all M14 SBS and B44 SBS buses by November of 2019.

payload.claim_text: Automated Bus Lane Enforcement (ABLE) is now active on all M15 SBS buses, and will roll out on all M14 SBS and B44 SBS buses by November of 2019.

tool_args.label: Bx5 and Bx6 SBS combined frequency on Story Avenue
tool_args.raw_text: With both the Bx5 and Bx6 SBS combined, Story Avenue will now have a bus scheduled every 3 minutes in the weekday AM peak, every 6 minutes in the midday, and every 4 minutes in the PM peak.

payload.claim_text: With both the Bx5 and Bx6 SBS combined, Story Avenue will now have a bus scheduled every 3 minutes in the weekday AM peak, every 6 minutes in the midday, and every 4 minutes in the PM peak.

tool_args.label: Network Redesign Goals claim
tool_args.raw_text: We used the following tactics: Increased frequency of buses by building an all-day frequent network, Created more direct routings, Balanced bus stop spacing, Enhanced connectivity, Expanded bus priority

payload.claim_text: Increased frequency of buses by building an all-day frequent network, Created more direct routings, Balanced bus stop spacing, Enhanced connectivity, Expanded bus priority

tool_args.label: All-day frequent network definition
tool_args.raw_text: All-day bus service is defined as bus service that operates consistently throughout the day and into the evening. From 7:00am to 9:00pm. So, we bolstered frequency during these off-peak periods to provide frequent service consistently throughout the day.

payload.claim_text: All-day bus service is defined as bus service that operates consistently throughout the day and into the evening. From 7:00am to 9:00pm. Bolstered frequency during off-peak periods to provide frequent service consistently throughout the day.

tool_args.raw_text: Summer/Fall 2020 - Plan to Be Implemented

tool_args.label: Bx15 split and M125 creation
tool_args.raw_text: The existing Bx15 will be split into two routes to improve reliability and bus speeds. The new Bx15 Local and Limited service will operate between The Hub and Fordham Plaza at all times. The new M125 route will replace service along 125 Street to The Hub.

payload.claim_text: The existing Bx15 will be split into two routes. The new Bx15 Local and Limited service will operate between The Hub and Fordham Plaza at all times. The new M125 route will replace service along 125 Street to The Hub.

tool_args.label: Bx36 routing change to East Tremont Avenue
tool_args.raw_text: The new Bx36 will still travel between Soundview and Washington Heights, but will now operate on East Tremont Avenue instead of East 174 and East 180 Streets. It will avoid its existing indirect routing along Boston Road.

payload.claim_text: Bx36 will now operate on East Tremont Avenue instead of East 174 and East 180 Streets, avoid its existing indirect routing along Boston Road.

tool_args.label: Bx6 SBS reroute to Story Avenue / Turnbull and Pugsley
tool_args.raw_text: The Bx6 Select Bus Service will be rerouted along Bruckner Boulevard, Bronx River Avenue, and Story Avenue to a new eastern terminal at Turnbull and Pugsley avenues. The route will no longer serve Hunts Point, which will continue to be served by Bx6 Local Service.

payload.claim_text: Bx6 SBS will be rerouted along Bruckner Boulevard, Bronx River Avenue, and Story Avenue to a new eastern terminal at Turnbull and Pugsley avenues. The route will no longer serve Hunts Point, which will continue to be served by Bx6 Local Service.

tool_args.label: Bx40 and Bx42 routing change via East Tremont Avenue and East 180 Street
tool_args.raw_text: Bx40 and Bx42 will travel between Throgs Neck and Morris Heights via East Tremont Avenue and East 180 Street. The rerouted Bx36 will replace existing routing on Rosedale and Webster avenues.

payload.claim_text: Bx40 and Bx42 will travel via East Tremont Avenue and East 180 Street. Bx36 replaces existing Bx40/42 routing on Rosedale and Webster avenues. New connection to E 180 St 2 5 station (accessible).

tool_args.label: M100 routing change - discontinue 125 Street service
tool_args.raw_text: M100 routing will change. Service along 125 Street will be discontinued. New southbound terminal at Amsterdam Avenue and West 125 Street.

payload.claim_text: M100 service along 125 Street discontinued. New southbound terminal at Amsterdam Avenue and West 125 Street.

tool_args.label: Bx40 Throgs Neck Draft Plan proposal not implemented
tool_args.raw_text: The Draft Plan proposal to adjust Bx40 service in Throgs Neck will not be implemented.

payload.claim_text: Draft Plan proposal to adjust Bx40 service in Throgs Neck will not be implemented. The southeastern route alignments of the Bx40 and Bx42 will remain as they exist today in Throgs Neck.

tool_args.label: Bx1 stop removal: 3% of stops removed
tool_args.raw_text: Three percent (3 of 93) of the Bx1 stops will be removed, improving the average distance between stops from 861 feet to 890 feet.

tool_args.label: Bx40 stop removal: 32% of stops removed
tool_args.raw_text: Thirty-two percent (36 of 111) of Bx40 stops along the new alignment will be removed, improving stop spacing from 800 feet to 1,190 feet.

tool_args.label: Bx36 stop removal: 21% of stops removed
tool_args.raw_text: Twenty-one percent (18 of 86) of Bx36 stops along the new alignment will be removed, improving stop spacing from 795 feet to 1,009 feet.

tool_args.label: Bx15 stop removal: 21% of stops removed
tool_args.raw_text: Twenty-one percent (8 of 39) of the Bx15 stops along the new alignment will be removed, improving stop spacing from 967 feet to 1,225 feet.

tool_args.label: Bx42 stop removal: 27% of stops removed
tool_args.raw_text: Twenty-seven percent (31 of 114) of Bx42 stops along the new alignment will be removed, improving stop spacing from 807 feet to 1,112 feet.

tool_args.label: Bx5 stop removal: 13% of stops removed
tool_args.raw_text: Thirteen percent (9 of 69) of the Bx5 stops will be removed, improving the average distance between stops from 1,154 feet to 1,330 feet.

tool_args.label: Bx1 Limited stop removal
tool_args.raw_text: Three percent (2 of 53) of the Bx1 Limited stops will be removed, improving the average distance between Limited stops from 1,523 feet to 1,584 feet.

tool_args.label: Bx3 stop removal: 14%
tool_args.raw_text: Fourteen percent (8 of 58) of the Bx3 stops will be removed, improving the average distance between stops from 813 feet to 946 feet.

tool_args.label: Bx4 stop removal: 10%
tool_args.raw_text: Ten percent (6 of 60) of the Bx4 stops will be removed, improving the average distance between stops from 808 feet to 900 feet.

tool_args.label: Bx4A stop removal: 19%
tool_args.raw_text: Nineteen percent (9 of 48) of the Bx4A stops along the new alignment will be removed, improving stop spacing from 762 feet to 943 feet.
